No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted in part; denied in part. The motion to preclude the depositions is denied. Plaintiff has the right to pursue this discovery to support her punitive damages claims. The motion is granted in so far as to confirm that the testimony and documents requested must relate to the purposes stated in Plaintiff's opposition, i.e. the training of the employees involved in the accident. While James Negrete was not driving the ambulance, the allegations are that he was involved in the decision to run code 3 through the red light, so his training is also relevant regarding punitives. Given the upcoming trial date, counsel shall work cooperatively to schedule the depositions such that they are completed by April 17, 2023.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF YOUSEF GHEITH's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages As To Defendant Natalie Annette Gonzales.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to file an amended complaint to plead a claim for punitive damages is conditionally granted. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment. (See Hirsa v. Superior Court (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-489.) This ruling giving leave to amend is conditioned on plaintiff stipulating that defendant can bring a motion for summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages, if warranted, on 45-calendar days notice. The opposition to such a motion would be due 15 calendar days prior to the hearing and the reply would be due 6 calendar days prior to the hearing.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/VP)
